en: Then Naomi her mother-in-law said to her, "My daughter, should I not seek rest for you, that it may be well with you?
zh: 路得的婆婆拿俄米對她說：“女兒啊，我不是應當為你找個歸宿，使你得到幸福嗎？

en: Is not Boaz our relative, with whose young women you were? See, he is winnowing barley tonight at the threshing floor.
zh: 你常常和他的女傭人在一起的波阿斯不是我們的親戚嗎？他今天晚上要在禾場上簸大麥。

en: Wash therefore and anoint yourself, and put on your cloak and go down to the threshing floor, but do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking.
zh: 你洗個澡，抹上香膏，穿上斗篷，就下到禾場去。不過，那個人還沒有吃喝完畢，別讓他認出你來。

en: But when he lies down, observe the place where he lies. Then go and uncover his feet and lie down, and he will tell you what to do."
zh: 等到他躺下來，你要弄清楚他所躺的地方，進去掀露他的腳，然後躺下去。他就會告訴你該作甚麼。”

en: And she replied, "All that you say I will do."
zh: 路得對她說：“你吩咐的，我就去作。”

en: So she went down to the threshing floor and did just as her mother-in-law had commanded her.
zh: 於是，她下到禾場去，照著婆婆吩咐的一切去作。

en: And when Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. Then she came softly and uncovered his feet and lay down.
zh: 波阿斯吃喝完了，心裡舒暢，就去躺在麥堆的旁邊。路得悄悄地來，掀露他的腳，躺臥下去。

en: At midnight the man was startled and turned over, and behold, a woman lay at his feet!
zh: 到了夜半，波阿斯驚醒過來，一翻身就看到一個女子躺在他的腳邊，

en: He said, "Who are you?" And she answered, "I am Ruth, your servant. Spread your wings over your servant, for you are a redeemer."
zh: 就說：“你是誰？”她回答：“我是你的婢女路得，請用你的衣襟遮蓋你的婢女，因為你是我一個有買贖權的近親。”

en: And he said, "May you be blessed by the LORD, my daughter. You have made this last kindness greater than the first in that you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich.
zh: 波阿斯說：“我女兒，願你蒙耶和華賜福。你末後表現的愛心比起初更大，因為年輕人無論貧富，你都沒有跟從。

en: And now, my daughter, do not fear. I will do for you all that you ask, for all my fellow townsmen know that you are a worthy woman.
zh: 我女兒，現在你不要怕，你所說的，我一定去作。本城的人都知道你是個賢慧的女人。

en: And now it is true that I am a redeemer. Yet there is a redeemer nearer than I.
zh: 不錯，我是你那有買贖權的近親，可惜還有一個有買贖權的近親比我更親。

en: Remain tonight, and in the morning, if he will redeem you, good; let him do it. But if he is not willing to redeem you, then, as the LORD lives, I will redeem you. Lie down until the morning."
zh: 今晚你就在這裡過夜，明早如果他肯盡買贖你的本分，好，就由他來履行；假如他不願意這樣作，我指著永活的耶和華起誓，我一定盡買贖你的本分。你只管躺到天亮吧。”

en: So she lay at his feet until the morning, but arose before one could recognize another. And he said, "Let it not be known that the woman came to the threshing floor."
zh: 路得躺在波阿斯腳邊直到早晨。因為波阿斯說過不可以讓人知道有女人來過禾場，路得就趁天色未明，人們彼此無法辨認的時候就起來。

en: And he said, "Bring the garment you are wearing and hold it out." So she held it, and he measured out six measures of barley and put it on her. Then she went into the city.
zh: 他又說：“拿你所披的外衣來，把它打開。”她打開了，他就量了六簸箕大麥，放在她的肩上。路得就進城去。

en: And when she came to her mother-in-law, she said, "How did you fare, my daughter?" Then she told her all that the man had done for her,
zh: 到她婆婆那裡，婆婆就問：“我女兒，怎樣了？”路得就把那人對她所作的一切都告訴了婆婆，

en: saying, "These six measures of barley he gave to me, for he said to me, 'You must not go back empty-handed to your mother-in-law.'"
zh: 又說：“他給我這六簸箕大麥，對我說：‘不要空手去見你婆婆。’”

en: She replied, "Wait, my daughter, until you learn how the matter turns out, for the man will not rest but will settle the matter today."
zh: 拿俄米說：“我女兒，你儘管安靜等候，直到你知道事情怎樣了結，因為那人今天不把事情辦妥，絕不歇息。”
